1→# UX Competitor Analysis - Railway Maintenance Scheduling Software
     2→
     3→> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-08
     4→> **Purpose**: Steal best ideas from competitors - "Good artists copy, great artists steal"
     5→> **Sources**: Web research via Anthropic Claude
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. IBM Maximo Scheduler
    10→
    11→### Overview
    12→IBM's enterprise asset management system, widely used in rail/metro operations including Dubai Metro.
    13→
    14→### Key UI Features
    15→
    16→#### Gantt Chart Capabilities
    17→- **Drag-and-drop scheduling**: Modify and mass-update work orders directly in Gantt view
    18→- **Task dependencies**: Visual critical path with float calculation
    19→- **Resource histogram**: Below Gantt showing resource loading vs availability
    20→- **Multiple views**: Day, week, month, quarter, year timeline views
    21→- **PM forecasting**: Gantt view for preventive maintenance with easy drag-drop adjustments
    22→
    23→#### Planning Views
    24→- **Graphical Scheduling**: Long to short term planning with Gantt + histogram
    25→- **Work View & Assignment View**: Both Gantt-based for supervisors
    26→- **Dispatch View**: Day calendar + map showing labor/crew assignments with travel time
    27→
    28→#### Railway-Specific Features
    29→- **Consist management**: For railway assets
    30→- **Dynamic segmentation**: For Railways, Roads, Pipelines
    31→- **Repair facility assignment**: Short-term planning for garage/railway shed assignment
    32→
    33→### Prometheus Group Enhancement (for Maximo)
    34→- **Four-quadrant Gantt layout**: Flexible view
    35→- **Real-time capacity effects**: See resource impact instantly
    36→- **Direct Edit or Mass Change**: For orders and operations
    37→
    38→### Weaknesses to Exploit
    39→- Fragmented into role-based apps (our opportunity: unified interface)
    40→- Struggles with 10k+ records (our target: 60fps with 60k tasks)
    41→- Discrete day/week/month tabs (our advantage: continuous semantic zoom)
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## 2. Railnova (Brussels, Belgium)
    46→
    47→### Overview
    48→Fleet management + telematics for rail industry. Clients include Eurotunnel, Alstom, Lineas, SNCF, DB Cargo.
    49→
    50→### Products
    51→
    52→#### Railfleet (ECM Fleet Management)
    53→- **Operational Dashboard**: All fleet info in one place
    54→  - Asset status
    55→  - Restrictions
    56→  - Rolling stock location
    57→  - Key telematic values
    58→  - Events and availability notifications
    59→
    60→- **Planning Dashboard**: Complete overview of maintenance events
    61→  - Event states: unplanned, planned, overdue, closed
    62→  - Per-asset filtering
    63→  - Bundle corrective + preventive into interventions
    64→
    65→- **Maintenance Planning Dashboard**:
    66→  - Filter events by type
    67→  - Schedule new maintenance interventions
    68→  - Bundle based on open events
    69→
    70→- **Digital Train Logbook**:
    71→  - Real-time tablet view for drivers
    72→  - Train availability and condition
    73→  - Operational status and restrictions
    74→  - Recent events list
    75→
    76→#### Railgenius (Real-time Monitoring)
    77→- **Live Monitoring Module**:
    78→  - Real-time telematics from all assets
    79→  - Color-coded values for anomaly detection
    80→  - Predictive alerts via detection rules
    81→
    82→- **Telematics Data Dashboard**:
    83→  - Historical and real-time data
    84→  - Usage pattern analysis
    85→  - Fault code occurrence reporting
    86→
    87→### Integration Capabilities
    88→- Web services API
    89→- IBM Maximo, MySQL Server, .Net, Oracle, SAP integration
    90→
    91→### UI Patterns to Steal
    92→- Color-coded anomaly indicators (spot abnormal values instantly)
    93→- Event state pipeline (unplanned → planned → overdue → closed)
    94→- Digital logbook concept for train-level status
    95→- Bundling corrective + preventive events
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→## 3. SignatureRail (UK, Modaxo Group)
   100→
   101→### Overview
   102→Train timetable and resource planning since 1994. Global presence with TrainPlan flagship product.
   103→
   104→### TrainPlan Features
   105→- Timetable generation considering all resources and constraints
   106→- Optimize capacity
   107→- Reduce vehicle and operator costs
   108→- Improve services
   109→
   110→### User Testimonial (Metro-North Railroad)
   111→> "TrainPlan allows us to manage our data in an efficient manner allowing for safe and high performing service changes to be implemented as quickly as possible."
   112→
   113→### Marey Chart Visualization (Industry Gold Standard)
   114→
   115→#### What is a Marey Chart?
   116→- Invented by Étienne-Jules Marey (1878) based on Mr. Ibry's method
   117→- **Axes**: Distance (Y) vs Time (X)
   118→- **Trains**: Diagonal lines - slope = speed
   119→- **Direction**: Top-left to bottom-right (one direction), bottom-left to top-right (reverse)
   120→- **Stops**: Horizontal sections indicate stop duration
   121→- **Crossings**: Lines crossing = trains passing each other
   122→
   123→#### Why Marey Charts are Superior
   124→- "Far superior representation for finding late trains and understanding delay propagation"
   125→- Both time and space dimensions in compact form
   126→- Used as cover of Tufte's "The Visual Display of Quantitative Information"
   127→- Speed immediately visible from line slope
   128→
   129→#### Modern Implementations
   130→- [D3.js Observable Example](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains)
   131→- [JointJS Demo](https://www.jointjs.com/demos/marey-chart)
   132→- [Tableau Public](https://public.tableau.com/en-us/s/blog/2015/08/marey-inspired-path-train-schedule)
   133→
   134→### UI Patterns to Steal
   135→- Marey chart for train scheduling visualization
   136→- What-if scenario management
   137→- Constraint-aware scheduling
   138→
   139→---
   140→
   141→## 4. Other Notable Competitors
   142→
   143→### PTV Visum
   144→- **Scenario management**: Create and manage infrastructure modifications
   145→- **Fleet planning**: Depot capacities, turning times, empty runs
   146→- **Batch evaluation**: Compare scenarios programmatically
   147→
   148→### TBEST (Free Desktop Software)
   149→- **GIS-based**: Integrates socio-economic, land use, transit network
   150→- **Scenario planning**: True network-based future year simulation
   151→
   152→### AnyLogic
   153→- **Simulation-based optimization**: Two-layer approach
   154→- **What-if scenarios**: Assess economic effects of changes
   155→- **Replacement service planning**: For scheduled shutdowns
   156→
   157→### Tractian CMMS
   158→- **AI-driven workload balancing**: Eliminates scheduling conflicts
   159→- **Real-time technician availability**: Auto task prioritization
   160→- **Visual Scheduling**: Plan by technician, area, or shift
   161→- **Overload detection**: See idle time instantly
   162→
   163→### FaultFixers
   164→- **Drag-and-drop calendar**: Intuitive ticket assignment
   165→- **Gantt Chart view**: See everyone's schedule at once
   166→
   167→---
   168→
   169→## 5. Transit Dashboard Best Practices
   170→
   171→### Real-World Examples
   172→
   173→#### WMATA (Washington Metro)
   174→- **MetroPulse**: Real-time headway and schedule adherence
   175→- **Trains in service**: Live count
   176→- **Service Excellence Dashboard**: Rail, Bus, MetroAccess data
   177→
   178→#### CTE KPI Platform
   179→- **Single pane of glass**: Track progress, spot trends
   180→- **Automated API feeds**: Vehicle telematics integration
   181→- **Custom data inputs**: Flexible reporting
   182→
   183→### Common Transit KPIs
   184→- Miles driven
   185→- Drop sequence
   186→- Number of stops
   187→- Arrival/departure times
   188→- DOT reportable incidents
   189→- Speeding incidents
   190→- Cost-per-mile
   191→- Revenue-per-mile
   192→- Miles per gallon
   193→- Vehicle idle time
   194→- On-time performance
   195→- Missed trips
   196→- Prediction accuracy
   197→
   198→---
   199→
   200→## 6. Gestalt Principles for Dashboard Design
   201→
   202→### Core Principles
   203→
   204→1. **Proximity**
   205→   - Group related filters together
   206→   - Place filter near its affected view
   207→   - Example: Fleet status filters grouped with fleet list
   208→
   209→2. **Similarity**
   210→   - Same colors for same data types
   211→   - Consistent shapes for similar metrics
   212→   - Example: All "fit" trains in green, all "unfit" in red
   213→
   214→3. **Enclosure**
   215→   - Cards/boxes for related metrics
   216→   - Clear distinction between data sets
   217→   - Example: KPI cards with distinct borders
   218→
   219→4. **Connectedness**
   220→   - Lines linking related elements
   221→   - Network and line graph patterns
   222→   - Example: Dependency lines between tasks
   223→
   224→5. **Closure**
   225→   - Minimal axis lines (just X and Y enough)
   226→   - User perceives complete structure
   227→   - Example: Bar charts without full enclosure
   228→
   229→6. **Continuity**
   230→   - Sort bars large to small
   231→   - Time charts past to future
   232→   - Perceived as continuous whole
   233→
   234→### Application to MMS
   235→- Group maintenance metrics together (proximity)
   236→- Color-code by fleet type and task status (similarity)
   237→- Use cards for KPIs with clear borders (enclosure)
   238→- Show task dependencies with connecting lines (connectedness)
   239→- Minimal chart decoration (closure)
   240→- Chronological timeline (continuity)
   241→
   242→---
   243→
   244→## 7. Calendar Heatmap Patterns
   245→
   246→### When to Use
   247→- Daily values are important
   248→- Want to view whole year at once
   249→- Spot patterns at month/week/day level
   250→- Compare weekdays vs weekends
   251→
   252→### When NOT to Use
   253→- Need trend/seasonality analysis (use line charts)
   254→- Monthly/yearly aggregate trends
   255→- Simple KPI tracking
   256→
   257→### Best Practices
   258→- Choose readable colors even with dark shades
   259→- Use continuous date calendar table
   260→- Common layouts: 4x3, 3x4, 6x2 grids
   261→- Color intensity = value magnitude
   262→
   263→### MMS Application
   264→- **MACRO view**: Calendar heatmap showing maintenance density
   265→- **Color scale**: Viridis (low=yellow → high=purple)
   266→- **Drill-down**: Click month/week to zoom MESO level
   267→
   268→---
   269→
   270→## 8. MMS Differentiators (vs Competitors)
   271→
   272→| Feature | Competitors | MMS |
   273→|---------|-------------|-----|
   274→| Zoom | Discrete tabs (day/week/month) | Continuous semantic zoom |
   275→| Performance | Maximo struggles at 10k | 60fps with 60k tasks |
   276→| Interface | Fragmented role-based apps | Unified single canvas |
   277→| Conflict resolution | Manual identification | Visual drag-drop with instant feedback |
   278→| Scenario mode | Separate planning modules | Inline sandbox with violet visual |
   279→| Chart type | Gantt only | Gantt + Marey + Heatmap (LOD) |
   280→
   281→---
   282→
   283→## 9. Features to Steal (Priority Order)
   284→
   285→### HIGH PRIORITY
   286→1. Drag-and-drop Gantt scheduling (IBM Maximo, Tractian)
   287→2. Color-coded anomaly indicators (Railnova)
   288→3. Event state pipeline (unplanned → planned → overdue → closed)
   289→4. Resource histogram below schedule (Maximo)
   290→5. Marey chart for train visualization (SignatureRail)
   291→
   292→### MEDIUM PRIORITY
   293→6. Digital train logbook per asset (Railnova)
   294→7. Four-quadrant flexible layout (Prometheus)
   295→8. Scenario comparison with batch evaluation (PTV Visum)
   296→9. What-if scenario with economic impact (AnyLogic)
   297→10. Calendar heatmap for maintenance density
   298→
   299→### FUTURE CONSIDERATION
   300→11. AI-driven workload balancing (Tractian)
   301→12. GIS integration (TBEST)
   302→13. Predictive maintenance alerts (Railgenius)
   303→14. Mobile dispatch interface (Railnova)
   304→
   305→---
   306→
   307→## Sources
   308→
   309→- [IBM Maximo Scheduler - Prometheus Group](https://www.prometheusgroup.com/solutions/planning-and-scheduling-ibm-maximo)
   310→- [Railnova Official Site](https://www.railnova.eu/)
   311→- [Railfleet ECM Platform](https://www.railnova.eu/products/railfleet/)
   312→- [SignatureRail Planning Solutions](https://signaturerail.com/planning-solutions/)
   313→- [Marey's Trains - D3 Observable](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains)
   314→- [Gestalt Principles in Dashboard Design](https://playfairdata.com/applying-gestalt-principles-to-dashboard-design/)
   315→- [WMATA Service Excellence Dashboard](https://www.wmata.com/initiatives/open-data-hub/Metro-Service-Excellence-Dashboard.cfm)
   316→- [Tractian CMMS](https://tractian.com/en/cmms/planning-and-scheduling)
   317→- [PTV Visum](https://www.ptvgroup.com/en-us/products/ptv-visum)

# UX Competitor Analysis - Railway Maintenance Scheduling Software

> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-08
> **Purpose**: Steal best ideas from competitors - "Good artists copy, great artists steal"
> **Sources**: Web research via Anthropic Claude

---

## 1. IBM Maximo Scheduler

### Overview
IBM's enterprise asset management system, widely used in rail/metro operations including Dubai Metro.

### Key UI Features

#### Gantt Chart Capabilities
- **Drag-and-drop scheduling**: Modify and mass-update work orders directly in Gantt view
- **Task dependencies**: Visual critical path with float calculation
- **Resource histogram**: Below Gantt showing resource loading vs availability
- **Multiple views**: Day, week, month, quarter, year timeline views
- **PM forecasting**: Gantt view for preventive maintenance with easy drag-drop adjustments

#### Planning Views
- **Graphical Scheduling**: Long to short term planning with Gantt + histogram
- **Work View & Assignment View**: Both Gantt-based for supervisors
- **Dispatch View**: Day calendar + map showing labor/crew assignments with travel time

#### Railway-Specific Features
- **Consist management**: For railway assets
- **Dynamic segmentation**: For Railways, Roads, Pipelines
- **Repair facility assignment**: Short-term planning for garage/railway shed assignment

### Prometheus Group Enhancement (for Maximo)
- **Four-quadrant Gantt layout**: Flexible view
- **Real-time capacity effects**: See resource impact instantly
- **Direct Edit or Mass Change**: For orders and operations

### Weaknesses to Exploit
- Fragmented into role-based apps (our opportunity: unified interface)
- Struggles with 10k+ records (our target: 60fps with 60k tasks)
- Discrete day/week/month tabs (our advantage: continuous semantic zoom)

---

## 2. Railnova (Brussels, Belgium)

### Overview
Fleet management + telematics for rail industry. Clients include Eurotunnel, Alstom, Lineas, SNCF, DB Cargo.

### Products

#### Railfleet (ECM Fleet Management)
- **Operational Dashboard**: All fleet info in one place
  - Asset status
  - Restrictions
  - Rolling stock location
  - Key telematic values
  - Events and availability notifications

- **Planning Dashboard**: Complete overview of maintenance events
  - Event states: unplanned, planned, overdue, closed
  - Per-asset filtering
  - Bundle corrective + preventive into interventions

- **Maintenance Planning Dashboard**:
  - Filter events by type
  - Schedule new maintenance interventions
  - Bundle based on open events

- **Digital Train Logbook**:
  - Real-time tablet view for drivers
  - Train availability and condition
  - Operational status and restrictions
  - Recent events list

#### Railgenius (Real-time Monitoring)
- **Live Monitoring Module**:
  - Real-time telematics from all assets
  - Color-coded values for anomaly detection
  - Predictive alerts via detection rules

- **Telematics Data Dashboard**:
  - Historical and real-time data
  - Usage pattern analysis
  - Fault code occurrence reporting

### Integration Capabilities
- Web services API
- IBM Maximo, MySQL Server, .Net, Oracle, SAP integration

### UI Patterns to Steal
- Color-coded anomaly indicators (spot abnormal values instantly)
- Event state pipeline (unplanned → planned → overdue → closed)
- Digital logbook concept for train-level status
- Bundling corrective + preventive events

---

## 3. SignatureRail (UK, Modaxo Group)

### Overview
Train timetable and resource planning since 1994. Global presence with TrainPlan flagship product.

### TrainPlan Features
- Timetable generation considering all resources and constraints
- Optimize capacity
- Reduce vehicle and operator costs
- Improve services

### User Testimonial (Metro-North Railroad)
> "TrainPlan allows us to manage our data in an efficient manner allowing for safe and high performing service changes to be implemented as quickly as possible."

### Marey Chart Visualization (Industry Gold Standard)

#### What is a Marey Chart?
- Invented by Étienne-Jules Marey (1878) based on Mr. Ibry's method
- **Axes**: Distance (Y) vs Time (X)
- **Trains**: Diagonal lines - slope = speed
- **Direction**: Top-left to bottom-right (one direction), bottom-left to top-right (reverse)
- **Stops**: Horizontal sections indicate stop duration
- **Crossings**: Lines crossing = trains passing each other

#### Why Marey Charts are Superior
- "Far superior representation for finding late trains and understanding delay propagation"
- Both time and space dimensions in compact form
- Used as cover of Tufte's "The Visual Display of Quantitative Information"
- Speed immediately visible from line slope

#### Modern Implementations
- [D3.js Observable Example](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains)
- [JointJS Demo](https://www.jointjs.com/demos/marey-chart)
- [Tableau Public](https://public.tableau.com/en-us/s/blog/2015/08/marey-inspired-path-train-schedule)

### UI Patterns to Steal
- Marey chart for train scheduling visualization
- What-if scenario management
- Constraint-aware scheduling

---

## 4. Other Notable Competitors

### PTV Visum
- **Scenario management**: Create and manage infrastructure modifications
- **Fleet planning**: Depot capacities, turning times, empty runs
- **Batch evaluation**: Compare scenarios programmatically

### TBEST (Free Desktop Software)
- **GIS-based**: Integrates socio-economic, land use, transit network
- **Scenario planning**: True network-based future year simulation

### AnyLogic
- **Simulation-based optimization**: Two-layer approach
- **What-if scenarios**: Assess economic effects of changes
- **Replacement service planning**: For scheduled shutdowns

### Tractian CMMS
- **AI-driven workload balancing**: Eliminates scheduling conflicts
- **Real-time technician availability**: Auto task prioritization
- **Visual Scheduling**: Plan by technician, area, or shift
- **Overload detection**: See idle time instantly

### FaultFixers
- **Drag-and-drop calendar**: Intuitive ticket assignment
- **Gantt Chart view**: See everyone's schedule at once

---

## 5. Transit Dashboard Best Practices

### Real-World Examples

#### WMATA (Washington Metro)
- **MetroPulse**: Real-time headway and schedule adherence
- **Trains in service**: Live count
- **Service Excellence Dashboard**: Rail, Bus, MetroAccess data

#### CTE KPI Platform
- **Single pane of glass**: Track progress, spot trends
- **Automated API feeds**: Vehicle telematics integration
- **Custom data inputs**: Flexible reporting

### Common Transit KPIs
- Miles driven
- Drop sequence
- Number of stops
- Arrival/departure times
- DOT reportable incidents
- Speeding incidents
- Cost-per-mile
- Revenue-per-mile
- Miles per gallon
- Vehicle idle time
- On-time performance
- Missed trips
- Prediction accuracy

---

## 6. Gestalt Principles for Dashboard Design

### Core Principles

1. **Proximity**
   - Group related filters together
   - Place filter near its affected view
   - Example: Fleet status filters grouped with fleet list

2. **Similarity**
   - Same colors for same data types
   - Consistent shapes for similar metrics
   - Example: All "fit" trains in green, all "unfit" in red

3. **Enclosure**
   - Cards/boxes for related metrics
   - Clear distinction between data sets
   - Example: KPI cards with distinct borders

4. **Connectedness**
   - Lines linking related elements
   - Network and line graph patterns
   - Example: Dependency lines between tasks

5. **Closure**
   - Minimal axis lines (just X and Y enough)
   - User perceives complete structure
   - Example: Bar charts without full enclosure

6. **Continuity**
   - Sort bars large to small
   - Time charts past to future
   - Perceived as continuous whole

### Application to MMS
- Group maintenance metrics together (proximity)
- Color-code by fleet type and task status (similarity)
- Use cards for KPIs with clear borders (enclosure)
- Show task dependencies with connecting lines (connectedness)
- Minimal chart decoration (closure)
- Chronological timeline (continuity)

---

## 7. Calendar Heatmap Patterns

### When to Use
- Daily values are important
- Want to view whole year at once
- Spot patterns at month/week/day level
- Compare weekdays vs weekends

### When NOT to Use
- Need trend/seasonality analysis (use line charts)
- Monthly/yearly aggregate trends
- Simple KPI tracking

### Best Practices
- Choose readable colors even with dark shades
- Use continuous date calendar table
- Common layouts: 4x3, 3x4, 6x2 grids
- Color intensity = value magnitude

### MMS Application
- **MACRO view**: Calendar heatmap showing maintenance density
- **Color scale**: Viridis (low=yellow → high=purple)
- **Drill-down**: Click month/week to zoom MESO level

---

## 8. MMS Differentiators (vs Competitors)

| Feature | Competitors | MMS |
|---------|-------------|-----|
| Zoom | Discrete tabs (day/week/month) | Continuous semantic zoom |
| Performance | Maximo struggles at 10k | 60fps with 60k tasks |
| Interface | Fragmented role-based apps | Unified single canvas |
| Conflict resolution | Manual identification | Visual drag-drop with instant feedback |
| Scenario mode | Separate planning modules | Inline sandbox with violet visual |
| Chart type | Gantt only | Gantt + Marey + Heatmap (LOD) |

---

## 9. Features to Steal (Priority Order)

### HIGH PRIORITY
1. Drag-and-drop Gantt scheduling (IBM Maximo, Tractian)
2. Color-coded anomaly indicators (Railnova)
3. Event state pipeline (unplanned → planned → overdue → closed)
4. Resource histogram below schedule (Maximo)
5. Marey chart for train visualization (SignatureRail)

### MEDIUM PRIORITY
6. Digital train logbook per asset (Railnova)
7. Four-quadrant flexible layout (Prometheus)
8. Scenario comparison with batch evaluation (PTV Visum)
9. What-if scenario with economic impact (AnyLogic)
10. Calendar heatmap for maintenance density

### FUTURE CONSIDERATION
11. AI-driven workload balancing (Tractian)
12. GIS integration (TBEST)
13. Predictive maintenance alerts (Railgenius)
14. Mobile dispatch interface (Railnova)

   543→
   544→## PatternFly 6 Integration Notes
   545→
   546→- Use PatternFly 6 tokens where available
   547→- Override with `--mms-*` tokens for custom values
   548→- Cards use `Elevation.TWO` shadow
   549→- Drawers position on right side for task details
   550→- Maintain dark theme as primary for canvas contrast
   551→

   553→
   554→## Performance Considerations
   555→
   556→- Canvas background: Pure black (`#0A0A0A`) for OLED power savings
   557→- Minimize color variety in heatmaps to improve GPU batching
   558→- Use opacity transitions over color transitions for smoother LOD
   559→- Prefer `transform` and `opacity` for animations (GPU-accelerated)

| **2-4 Weeks** | Simplified Gantt | Track occupancy, bottlenecks | Block movement |
| **1 Week** | Detailed Gantt | Status per train, mileage | Fine scheduling |
| **1-3 Days** | Capsules with details | Fitness, remaining km | Specific route assignment |

LAYER 2: CANVAS (WebGL/R3F)

```

```

---

## PatternFly 6 Integration Notes

- Use PatternFly 6 tokens where available
- Override with `--mms-*` tokens for custom values
- Cards use `Elevation.TWO` shadow
- Drawers position on right side for task details
- Maintain dark theme as primary for canvas contrast

---

## Performance Considerations

- Canvas background: Pure black (`#0A0A0A`) for OLED power savings
- Minimize color variety in heatmaps to improve GPU batching
- Use opacity transitions over color transitions for smoother LOD
- Prefer `transform` and `opacity` for animations (GPU-accelerated)

## 1. Main Dashboard (Primary View)

### Purpose
The primary workspace for maintenance planners. Shows the semantic zoom canvas with all 128 trains across 12 years.

### Components

#### 1.1 Masthead (PatternFly Masthead)

└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Zoom Controls**:
- Numeric percentage display (10% - 400%)
- +/- buttons with step (10% per click)
- Quick presets: Week, Month, Quarter, Year, All

**Mode Toggle**:
- Baseline (blue #0066CC): Production schedule
- Sandbox (violet #7C3AED): What-if scenarios
- Visual indicator when in sandbox mode

#### 1.2 Fleet Sidebar (PatternFly TreeView)

```

**Features**:
- Search/filter by train ID
- Fleet type toggle (All/Kinki/Alstom)
- Fitness status filter (Fit/Limited/Unfit)
- Click train to pan canvas to that row
- Color indicator: 🟢 Fit, 🟡 Limited, 🔴 Unfit
- Show maintenance due indicator (⏰)

#### 1.3 Main Canvas (React Three Fiber)

**LOD Levels**:

| Level | Zoom Range | Visualization |
|-------|------------|---------------|
| MACRO | < 0.5x | Heatmap (task density per month/train) |
| MESO | 0.5x - 2.0x | Task blocks (colored by type) |
| MICRO | > 2.0x | Task cards (with labels, details) |

**Interactions**:
- Pan: Click + drag
- Zoom: Scroll wheel (toward cursor)
- Select: Click on task
- Multi-select: Shift + click
- Drag (sandbox): Click + drag task to new date

**Visual Elements**:
- Grid lines (year boundaries, train rows)
- Today marker (red vertical line)
- Conflict indicators (red glow on overlapping tasks)
- Selection highlight (cyan border)
- Dependency lines (when visible)

#### 1.4 Minimap (Bottom-left overlay)
```

│ └────┘                 │
│ ▓▓▓░░░▓▓░░░▓▓▓░░ ← Heatmap │
└────────────────────────┘
```

**Features**:
- Shows full 12-year × 128 train overview
- Viewport rectangle (draggable)
- Click to jump to location
- Heatmap showing task density

#### 1.5 KPI Bar (Bottom-right, PatternFly Cards)
```
┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐
│ TASKS   │ │ CONFLICT│ │ FIT %   │ │ MILEAGE │
│  5,348  │ │   122   │ │   87%   │ │  +2.3%  │
│ ───────────│ ─────── │ ─────────│ ─────────│
│ +15 today │ │ ▼ -3   │ │ ▲ +2   │ │ vs pred │
└─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘
```

**Cards**:
1. **Tasks**: Total scheduled tasks (with delta)
2. **Conflicts**: Overlapping tasks (red if > 0)
3. **Fit %**: Trains fit for service (green/yellow/red)
4. **Mileage**: Variance vs predicted (with trend)

   627→### 3.2 Zoom Behavior
   628→
   629→#### 3.2.1 Mouse Wheel Zoom
   630→
   631→| Property | Value |
   632→|----------|-------|
   633→| Direction | Wheel up = zoom in, wheel down = zoom out |
   634→| Anchor | Zoom toward cursor position |
   635→| Sensitivity | 0.002 per wheel delta |
   636→| Min Zoom | 0.05x |
   637→| Max Zoom | 5.0x |
   638→| Easing | None (immediate) |
   639→
   640→**Algorithm:**
   641→```typescript
   642→// Zoom toward cursor
   643→const zoomFactor = Math.exp(-event.deltaY * sensitivity);
   644→const newZoom = clamp(currentZoom * zoomFactor, minZoom, maxZoom);
   645→
   646→// Adjust camera to keep cursor point fixed
   647→const cursorWorld = screenToWorld(cursor, camera);
   648→camera.x = cursor.x - cursorWorld.x * newZoom;
   649→camera.y = cursor.y - cursorWorld.y * newZoom;
   650→camera.zoom = newZoom;
   651→```
   652→
   653→#### 3.2.2 Pinch-to-Zoom (Touch)
   654→
   655→| Property | Value |
   656→|----------|-------|
   657→| Anchor | Center point between two fingers |
   658→| Sensitivity | Direct 1:1 mapping |
   659→| Threshold | 10px movement before initiating |
   660→
   661→#### 3.2.3 Double-Click Zoom
   662→
   663→| Action | Behavior |
   664→|--------|----------|
   665→| Double-click | Zoom in 2x toward click position |
   666→| Ctrl + Double-click | Zoom out 2x from click position |
   667→| Animation | 250ms ease-out |
   668→
   669→#### 3.2.4 Keyboard Zoom
   670→
   671→| Key | Action |
   672→|-----|--------|
   673→| `+` or `=` | Zoom in 1.25x |
   674→| `-` | Zoom out 0.8x |
   675→| `0` or `Ctrl+0` | Reset to 1.0x |
   676→| `Home` | Fit all content |
   677→
   678→### 3.3 Pan Behavior
   679→
   680→#### 3.3.1 Mouse Pan
   681→
   682→| Property | Value |
   683→|----------|-------|
   684→| Activation | Click and drag on canvas |
   685→| Cursor | `grab` (idle), `grabbing` (panning) |
   686→| Bounds | Elastic bounce at edges |
   687→
   688→#### 3.3.2 Inertia Scrolling
   689→
   690→| Property | Value |
   691→|----------|-------|
   692→| Initial Velocity | Last 3 frames average |
   693→| Friction | 0.95 per frame |
   694→| Stop Threshold | velocity < 0.1px/frame |
   695→
   696→**Algorithm:**
   697→```typescript
   698→// On mouse up
   699→const velocity = calculateAverageVelocity(last3Frames);
   700→
   701→// Animation loop
   702→function applyMomentum() {
   703→  if (Math.abs(velocity.x) < 0.1 && Math.abs(velocity.y) < 0.1) {
   704→    return; // Stop
   705→  }
   706→
   707→  camera.x += velocity.x;
   708→  camera.y += velocity.y;
   709→  velocity.x *= friction;
   710→  velocity.y *= friction;
   711→
   712→  requestAnimationFrame(applyMomentum);
   713→}
   714→```
   715→
   716→#### 3.3.3 Edge Bounce
   717→
   718→When panning beyond content bounds:
   719→- Elastic resistance increases with distance
   720→- Spring-back animation on release
   721→- Duration: 200ms
   722→- Easing: `easeOutBack`
   723→
   724→#### 3.3.4 Keyboard Pan
   725→
   726→| Key | Action |
   727→|-----|--------|
   728→| Arrow keys | Pan 100px in direction |
   729→| Shift + Arrow | Pan 500px in direction |
   730→| Page Up/Down | Pan one viewport height |
   731→
   732→### 3.4 Selection Behavior
   733→
   734→#### 3.4.1 Single Selection
   735→
   736→| Action | Behavior |
   737→|--------|----------|
   738→| Click task | Select task, deselect previous |
   739→| Click empty | Deselect all |
   740→| Click selected | Keep selected (for dragging) |
   741→
   742→#### 3.4.2 Multi-Selection
   743→
   744→| Action | Behavior |
   745→|--------|----------|
   746→| Ctrl + Click | Toggle task in selection |
   747→| Shift + Click | Select range (first to clicked) |
   748→| Ctrl + A | Select all visible tasks |
   749→| Escape | Deselect all |
   750→
   751→#### 3.4.3 Selection Rectangle (Marquee)
   752→
   753→| Property | Value |
   754→|----------|-------|
   755→| Activation | Click + drag on empty canvas |
   756→| Visual | Dashed blue rectangle |
   757→| Color | `rgba(0, 102, 204, 0.3)` fill, `#0066CC` border |
   758→
   759→### 3.5 Drag-and-Drop
   760→
   761→#### 3.5.1 Drag Initiation
   762→
   763→| Property | Value |
   764→|----------|-------|
   765→| Threshold | 5px movement |
   766→| Delay | 100ms hold |
   767→| Visual | Task becomes semi-transparent (50%) |
   768→
   769→#### 3.5.2 Drop Zones
   770→
   771→| Zone Type | Visual Feedback |
   772→|-----------|-----------------|
   773→| Valid | Green highlight on row |
   774→| Invalid | Red highlight, cursor: not-allowed |
   775→| Snap Point | Blue guide line |
   776→
   777→#### 3.5.3 Invalid Drop (Spring-Back)
   778→
   779→When dropped on invalid position:
   780→1. Task glows red briefly (200ms)
   781→2. Animates back to original position
   782→3. Duration: 300ms
   783→4. Easing: `easeOutElastic`

# MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler) - UX Specification

> **Version:** 1.0.0
> **Last Updated:** 2026-01-07
> **Status:** SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for implementation
> **Target:** Dubai Metro - 128 trains x 12 years x ~60k tasks @ 60fps

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Application Shell Layout](#1-application-shell-layout)
2. [Component Specifications](#2-component-specifications)
3. [Interaction States](#3-interaction-states)
4. [Color Palette](#4-color-palette)
5. [Typography](#5-typography)
6. [Responsive Behavior](#6-responsive-behavior)
7. [Appendix: ASCII Wireframes](#appendix-ascii-wireframes)

---

## 1. Application Shell Layout

### 1.1 Overall Architecture

The application uses a **Three-Layer Sandwich** architecture:

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                        LAYER 3: HUD OVERLAY                            |
|   HTML/React - pointer-events: none (except interactive elements)      |
|   - Navbar, Sidebar, Timeline Header, Minimap, Tooltips, Panels        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                        LAYER 2: CANVAS (WebGL)                         |
|   PixiJS/R3F - Main rendering surface                                  |
|   - Task bars, dependency lines, heatmaps, LOD layers                  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                        LAYER 1: ENGINE                                 |
|   Background workers - constraint processing, data calculations        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### 1.2 Viewport Dimensions

| Breakpoint | Width | Layout |
|------------|-------|--------|
| Desktop XL | >= 1920px | Full layout, expanded sidebar |
| Desktop | >= 1280px | Full layout, collapsed sidebar by default |
| Tablet | 768px - 1279px | Collapsed sidebar, smaller minimap |
| Mobile | < 768px | Full-screen canvas, bottom sheet panels |

### 1.3 Region Dimensions (Desktop >= 1280px)

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                NAVBAR (56px height)                                |
+-------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------+
|             |                   TIMELINE HEADER (32px height)                      |
|   SIDEBAR   +---------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  (130px     |                                                                     |
|  collapsed) |                                                                     |
|             |                      MAIN CANVAS                                    |
|  (280px     |                  (flex: fills remaining space)                      |
|  expanded)  |                                                                     |
|             |                                                     +---------------+
|             |                                                     |   MINIMAP     |
|             |                                                     |  (180x120px)  |
+-------------+-----------------------------------------------------+---------------+
```

### 1.4 Z-Index Layering

| Layer | Z-Index | Contents |
|-------|---------|----------|
| Modals/Dialogs | 1000 | Confirmation dialogs, alerts |
| Dropdowns | 900 | Navbar dropdowns, context menus |
| Tooltips | 800 | Task tooltips, info hovers |
| Detail Panel | 700 | Right drawer (400px) |
| Minimap | 600 | Bottom-right navigation |
| Navbar | 500 | Top navigation bar |
| Sidebar | 400 | Left train list |
| Timeline Header | 300 | Top timeline ruler |
| Canvas | 0 | Main WebGL surface |

### 1.5 Dark Theme Colors (Application Shell)

| Region | Token | Value (Dark) | Usage |
|--------|-------|--------------|-------|
| App Background | `--pf-t--global--background--color--primary--default` | `#151515` | Main app background |
| Canvas Background | `--mms-bg-canvas` | `#0A0A0A` | WebGL canvas |
| Panel Background | `--mms-bg-panel` | `#1F1F1F` | Sidebar, navbar |
| Surface Background | `--mms-bg-surface` | `#292929` | Cards, elevated elements |
| Border Default | `--pf-t--global--border--color--default` | `#3D3D3D` | Dividers |

---

## 2. Component Specifications

### 2.1 Navbar

**Dimensions:** Full width x 56px height
**Background:** `--mms-bg-panel` (#1F1F1F)
**Border:** 1px solid `--pf-t--global--border--color--default` (bottom)

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [Logo]  |  [<] [100%] [>] [Fit] |  [Baseline|Scenario] |  Tasks: 12.4k | [...]  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
   ^              ^                         ^                   ^            ^
   |              |                         |                   |            |
 24x24px     Zoom Controls           Mode Toggle           KPI Badge    Settings
```

#### 2.1.1 Logo (Left Region)

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Position | Left, 16px padding |
| Size | 24px x 24px icon + text |
| Font | RedHatDisplay, 16px, semibold (700) |
| Color | `--pf-t--global--text--color--regular` |
| Text | "MMS" or "Metro Scheduler" |

#### 2.1.2 Zoom Controls (Center-Left Region)

| Element | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Container | Horizontal flex, gap: 8px |
| Zoom Out Button | 32px x 32px, icon: `minus`, border-radius: 4px |
| Zoom Indicator | Min-width: 60px, text-align: center, format: "125%" |
| Zoom In Button | 32px x 32px, icon: `plus`, border-radius: 4px |
| Fit-to-View Button | 32px x 32px, icon: `expand`, tooltip: "Fit all tasks" |
| Buttons Background | `--mms-bg-surface` hover: `--mms-bg-hover` |

#### 2.1.3 Mode Toggle (Center Region)

| State | Visual | Color |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Baseline (Active) | Solid button | `#0066CC` (PatternFly blue) |
| Baseline (Inactive) | Outline button | `--pf-t--global--border--color--default` |
| Scenario (Active) | Solid button | `#6753AC` (PatternFly purple) |
| Scenario (Inactive) | Outline button | `--pf-t--global--border--color--default` |

**Behavior:**
- Toggle switches between Baseline (production data) and Scenario (sandbox)
- Active mode shows solid background with icon
- Scenario mode adds subtle purple tint to canvas border

#### 2.1.4 KPI Indicators (Center-Right Region)

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| Tasks: 12,456 | Conflicts: 23 | Utilization: 87% |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| KPI | Format | Color Logic |
|-----|--------|-------------|
| Total Tasks | Number with commas | Static white |
| Conflicts | Number | Red if > 0, green if 0 |
| Resource Utilization | Percentage | Green 80-100%, Yellow 60-79%, Red <60% |

**Badge Styling:**
- Background: `rgba(255,255,255,0.1)`
- Border-radius: 16px (pill shape)
- Padding: 4px 12px
- Font: 12px, medium (500)

#### 2.1.5 Settings Dropdown (Right Region)

| Item | Icon | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| Theme | `moon`/`sun` | Toggle dark/light theme |
| Export | `download` | Export schedule as CSV/PDF |
| Settings | `cog` | Open settings modal |
| Help | `question-circle` | Open help documentation |

---

### 2.2 Train Sidebar (Left Panel)

**Dimensions:**
- Collapsed: 130px width
- Expanded: 280px width
- Height: Full viewport minus navbar (calc(100vh - 56px))

**Background:** `--mms-bg-panel` (#1F1F1F)
**Border:** 1px solid `--pf-t--global--border--color--default` (right)

#### 2.2.1 Collapsed State (130px)

```
+------------------+
| [>] Trains (128) |
+------------------+
| [T] ALT-001      |
| [T] ALT-002      |
| [T] ALT-003      |
| ...              |
| [T] KNK-050      |
+------------------+
```

| Element | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Header Height | 44px |
| Expand Button | 24px x 24px, icon: `chevron-right` |
| Header Text | "Trains (128)", 14px, semibold |
| Row Height | Synced with canvas: `pxPerTrain * zoom` (default: 24px) |
| Train Icon | 16px x 16px, color varies by asset type |
| Train ID | 12px, monospace (RedHatMono) |
| Hover State | Background: `--mms-bg-hover` |
| Selected State | Background: `--mms-bg-selected`, left border: 3px `--pf-t--global--color--brand--default` |

**Train Icon Colors:**
- ALSTOM: `#3B82F6` (blue)
- KINKI: `#F59E0B` (amber)

#### 2.2.2 Expanded State (280px)

```
+--------------------------------+
| [<] Trains (128)   [Search...] |
+--------------------------------+
| [T] ALT-001                    |
|     Alstom Metropolis          |
|     Status: In Service         |
+--------------------------------+
| [T] ALT-002                    |
|     Alstom Metropolis          |
|     Status: Maintenance        |
+--------------------------------+
```

| Element | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Search Input | Full width minus padding, height: 32px |
| Row Height | 72px (3 lines of info) |
| Primary Text | Train ID, 14px, semibold |
| Secondary Text | Fleet name, 12px, `--pf-t--global--text--color--subtle` |
| Status Badge | 10px, pill shape, color by status |

**Status Badge Colors:**
- In Service: `#3E8635` (green)
- Maintenance: `#F0AB00` (gold)
- Out of Service: `#C9190B` (red)

#### 2.2.3 Scroll Synchronization

The sidebar scroll position MUST be synchronized with the canvas Y position:

```typescript
// Sidebar scroll offset calculation
const sidebarScrollTop = camera.y * zoom;
```

---

### 2.3 Timeline Header

**Dimensions:** Full canvas width x 32px height
**Background:** `--mms-bg-panel` (#1F1F1F)
**Border:** 1px solid `--pf-t--global--border--color--default` (bottom)

#### 2.3.1 Display by Zoom Level

| Zoom Range | Primary Labels | Secondary Labels | Grid Lines |
|------------|----------------|------------------|------------|
| < 0.3x | Years (2024, 2025...) | None | Year boundaries |
| 0.3x - 0.7x | Years | Quarters (Q1, Q2...) | Quarter boundaries |
| 0.7x - 1.5x | Quarters | Months (Jan, Feb...) | Month boundaries |
| 1.5x - 3.0x | Months | Weeks (W1, W2...) | Week boundaries |
| > 3.0x | Weeks | Days (Mon, Tue...) | Day boundaries |

#### 2.3.2 Label Styling

| Label Type | Font | Color | Position |
|------------|------|-------|----------|
| Primary | 14px, semibold | `--pf-t--global--text--color--regular` | Baseline, left-aligned to boundary |
| Secondary | 11px, regular | `--pf-t--global--text--color--subtle` | Below primary, center-aligned |
| Today Marker | 11px, bold | `#0066CC` | Vertical line + "Today" label |

#### 2.3.3 Today Indicator

```
                    |
                 [Today]
                    |
                    v
```

- Vertical line: 2px width, `#0066CC`
- Label: "Today" in blue pill badge
- Extends full canvas height as dotted line

---

### 2.4 Semantic Zoom Canvas (Main Area)

**Position:** Fills remaining space after navbar, sidebar, and timeline header
**Background:** `--mms-bg-canvas` (#0A0A0A)

#### 2.4.1 LOD (Level of Detail) System

```
Zoom:  0.05  0.35  0.50         2.00  2.15  5.0
        |     |     |             |     |    |
MACRO   [=====|-----|             |     |    ]  100% opacity -> fade out
MESO          [-----|=============|-----|    ]  fade in -> 100% -> fade out
MICRO               |             [-----|====]  fade in -> 100% opacity
        |     |     |             |     |    |
        |     +-----+             +-----+    |
        |   crossfade            crossfade   |
        |     zone                 zone      |
```

| LOD Level | Zoom Range | Visualization | Opacity Calculation |
|-----------|------------|---------------|---------------------|
| MACRO | < 0.35 | Heatmap grid | 100% |
| MACRO->MESO | 0.35-0.50 | Crossfade | Linear interpolation |
| MESO | 0.50-2.00 | Task blocks (DNA) | 100% |
| MESO->MICRO | 2.00-2.15 | Crossfade | Linear interpolation |
| MICRO | > 2.15 | Full task cards | 100% |

#### 2.4.2 MACRO View (Heatmap)

**Zoom Range:** < 0.5x
**Cell Size:** 300px width x 24px height (30 days x 1 train)

```
+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+
| 2024  |       |       |       |       | 2025  |
+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+
|  [3]  |  [7]  |  [2]  |  [5]  |  [8]  |  [4]  | ALT-001
+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+
|  [5]  |  [2]  |  [9]  |  [3]  |  [1]  |  [6]  | ALT-002
+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+
|  [1]  |  [4]  |  [6]  |  [8]  |  [2]  |  [3]  | ALT-003
+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+
```

**Heatmap Color Scale (YlOrRd interpolator):**

| Density | Color | Hex |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Empty (0) | Light cream | `#FFFFD4` |
| Low (1-3) | Light yellow | `#FED98E` |
| Medium (4-6) | Orange | `#FE9929` |
| High (7-9) | Dark orange | `#D95F0E` |
| Very High (10+) | Red | `#993404` |

**Cell Behavior:**
- NOT pickable (no hover/click interaction)
- Shows task count as centered number (optional, zoom > 0.3x)

#### 2.4.3 MESO View (DNA Strips / Task Blocks)

**Zoom Range:** 0.5x - 2.0x

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                           Q1 2024                                 |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ALT-001 | [====MAINT====]        [==OVH==]      [INSP]           |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ALT-002 |      [=====MAINT=====]            [==OVH==]            |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ALT-003 | [INSP]   [=====MAINT=====]                   [INSP]    |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

**Task Block Styling:**

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Height | 18px (within 24px row) |
| Border Radius | 4px |
| Vertical Margin | 3px top/bottom |
| Minimum Width | 4px (tasks < 1 day) |

**Task Block Colors:**

| Task Type | Default | Hover | Selected |
|-----------|---------|-------|----------|
| Maintenance | `#3B82F6` | `#60A5FA` (+brightness) | `#3B82F6` + cyan border |
| Overhaul | `#F59E0B` | `#FBBF24` (+brightness) | `#F59E0B` + cyan border |
| Inspection | `#6B7280` | `#9CA3AF` (+brightness) | `#6B7280` + cyan border |
| Conflict | Red glow overlay | Intensified glow | Red glow + cyan border |

**Row Backgrounds (Alternating):**
- Even rows: `rgba(255,255,255,0.02)`
- Odd rows: `rgba(248,250,252,0.03)`

#### 2.4.4 MICRO View (Detailed Task Cards)

**Zoom Range:** > 2.0x

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                        Week 12, 2024                              |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ALT-001 |                                                         |
|         | +--------------------------------------------------+   |
|         | | [M] Preventive Maintenance PM2                   |   |
|         | | Mar 15 - Mar 22 (7 days)                         |   |
|         | | [Dependencies: 3] [Resources: 2]                 |   |
|         | +--------------------------------------------------+   |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

**Task Card Dimensions:**
- Height: 64px minimum, 80px with expanded details
- Width: Proportional to duration (pxPerDay * duration * zoom)
- Min Width: 120px (shows abbreviated info if smaller)
- Padding: 8px

**Task Card Content:**

| Element | Font | Position |
|---------|------|----------|
| Type Icon | 16px | Top-left |
| Task Title | 14px, semibold | Top, after icon |
| Date Range | 12px, regular | Below title |
| Duration Badge | 11px, pill | Top-right |
| Metadata Row | 11px, subtle | Bottom |

**Dependency Lines:**

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Color | `rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.8)` (violet) |
| Width | 2px |
| Style | Curved bezier path |
| Arrow | 6px triangle at endpoint |
| Hover | 3px width, full opacity |

**Grid Lines:**

| Type | Color | Width |
|------|-------|-------|
| Train Row Boundaries | `rgba(255,255,255,0.1)` | 1px |
| Week Boundaries | `rgba(255,255,255,0.05)` | 1px |
| Month Boundaries | `rgba(255,255,255,0.15)` | 1.5px |
| Year Boundaries | `rgba(255,255,255,0.25)` | 2px |

---

### 2.5 Minimap

**Dimensions:** 180px width x 120px height
**Position:** Bottom-right, 16px margin from edges
**Background:** `--mms-bg-surface` (#292929)
**Border:** 1px solid `--pf-t--global--border--color--default`
**Border Radius:** 8px
**Box Shadow:** `0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)`

```
+----------------------------------+
|  [X]  Minimap                    |
+----------------------------------+
|  +----------------------------+  |
|  | [Heatmap Grid 36x26]       |  |
|  |        +----+              |  |
|  |        |    | <- Viewport  |  |
|  |        +----+              |  |
|  +----------------------------+  |
+----------------------------------+
```

#### 2.5.1 Heatmap Grid

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Grid Cells | 36 columns (months) x ~26 rows (groups of 5 trains) |
| Cell Size | ~5px x ~4px |
| Color Scale | Same as MACRO view (YlOrRd) |

#### 2.5.2 Viewport Rectangle

|----------|-------|

| Fill | `rgba(0, 102, 204, 0.2)` |
| Corner Radius | 2px |

#### 2.5.3 Interactions

| Action | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| Click | Jump viewport center to clicked position |
| Drag rectangle | Pan main canvas |
| Scroll (inside minimap) | Zoom main canvas |
| Close button | Toggle minimap visibility |

### 2.6 Task Detail Panel (Right Drawer)

**Dimensions:** 400px width x full height

|                                        |

| +------------------------------------+ |

| Dependencies (3)                       |
| +------------------------------------+ |
| | -> Inspection INS-2341 (completed) | |

#### 2.6.1 Header

#### 2.6.2 Content Sections

| Train Information | Train icon, ID, fleet name, mileage |
| Schedule | Start/end dates, duration, assigned track |

#### 2.6.3 Action Buttons (Footer)

| Button | Style | Color | Action |

| Mark Complete | Secondary | Green | Marks task as done |
| Delete | Danger | Red | Confirmation then delete |

---

## 3. Interaction States

### 3.1 Task States

#### 3.1.1 Visual State Matrix

| State | Background | Border | Effects | Cursor |
|-------|------------|--------|---------|--------|
| Default | Task type color | None | None | default |
| Hover | +20% brightness | None | Slight scale (1.02) | pointer |
| Selected | Task type color | 2px cyan `#06B6D4` | Elevation shadow | pointer |
| Dragging | 50% opacity | 2px dashed | Drop shadow | grabbing |
| Conflict | Task type color | None | Red glow (pulsing) | pointer |
| Completed | Task type color | None | Strikethrough overlay | default |
| Disabled | 30% opacity | None | None | not-allowed |

#### 3.1.2 Conflict Glow Animation

```css
@keyframes conflict-pulse {
  0%, 100% {
    box-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(201, 25, 11, 0.4);
  }
  50% {
    box-shadow: 0 0 16px rgba(201, 25, 11, 0.7);
  }
}
```

- Duration: 2s
- Timing: ease-in-out
- Iteration: infinite

#### 3.1.3 Completed Task Overlay

```css
.task-completed::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
  height: 2px;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.6);
  transform: translateY(-50%);
}
```

### 3.2 Zoom Behavior

#### 3.2.1 Mouse Wheel Zoom

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Direction | Wheel up = zoom in, wheel down = zoom out |
| Anchor | Zoom toward cursor position |
| Sensitivity | 0.002 per wheel delta |
| Min Zoom | 0.05x |
| Max Zoom | 5.0x |
| Easing | None (immediate) |

**Algorithm:**
```typescript
// Zoom toward cursor
const zoomFactor = Math.exp(-event.deltaY * sensitivity);
const newZoom = clamp(currentZoom * zoomFactor, minZoom, maxZoom);

// Adjust camera to keep cursor point fixed
const cursorWorld = screenToWorld(cursor, camera);
camera.x = cursor.x - cursorWorld.x * newZoom;
camera.y = cursor.y - cursorWorld.y * newZoom;
camera.zoom = newZoom;
```

#### 3.2.2 Pinch-to-Zoom (Touch)

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Anchor | Center point between two fingers |
| Sensitivity | Direct 1:1 mapping |
| Threshold | 10px movement before initiating |

#### 3.2.3 Double-Click Zoom

| Action | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| Double-click | Zoom in 2x toward click position |
| Ctrl + Double-click | Zoom out 2x from click position |
| Animation | 250ms ease-out |

#### 3.2.4 Keyboard Zoom

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `+` or `=` | Zoom in 1.25x |
| `-` | Zoom out 0.8x |
| `0` or `Ctrl+0` | Reset to 1.0x |
| `Home` | Fit all content |

### 3.3 Pan Behavior

#### 3.3.1 Mouse Pan

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Activation | Click and drag on canvas |
| Cursor | `grab` (idle), `grabbing` (panning) |
| Bounds | Elastic bounce at edges |

#### 3.3.2 Inertia Scrolling

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Initial Velocity | Last 3 frames average |
| Friction | 0.95 per frame |
| Stop Threshold | velocity < 0.1px/frame |

**Algorithm:**
```typescript
// On mouse up
const velocity = calculateAverageVelocity(last3Frames);

// Animation loop
function applyMomentum() {
  if (Math.abs(velocity.x) < 0.1 && Math.abs(velocity.y) < 0.1) {
    return; // Stop
  }

  camera.x += velocity.x;
  camera.y += velocity.y;
  velocity.x *= friction;
  velocity.y *= friction;

  requestAnimationFrame(applyMomentum);
}
```

#### 3.3.3 Edge Bounce

When panning beyond content bounds:
- Elastic resistance increases with distance
- Spring-back animation on release
- Duration: 200ms
- Easing: `easeOutBack`

#### 3.3.4 Keyboard Pan

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Arrow keys | Pan 100px in direction |
| Shift + Arrow | Pan 500px in direction |
| Page Up/Down | Pan one viewport height |

#### 3.4.1 Single Selection

| Action | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| Click task | Select task, deselect previous |
| Click empty | Deselect all |
| Click selected | Keep selected (for dragging) |

#### 3.4.2 Multi-Selection

| Action | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| Ctrl + Click | Toggle task in selection |
| Shift + Click | Select range (first to clicked) |
| Ctrl + A | Select all visible tasks |

#### 3.4.3 Selection Rectangle (Marquee)

| Property | Value |

| Visual | Dashed blue rectangle |

### 3.5 Drag-and-Drop

#### 3.5.1 Drag Initiation

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Threshold | 5px movement |
| Delay | 100ms hold |
| Visual | Task becomes semi-transparent (50%) |

#### 3.5.2 Drop Zones

When dropped on invalid position:
1. Task glows red briefly (200ms)
2. Animates back to original position
3. Duration: 300ms
4. Easing: `easeOutElastic`

# MMS Navigation Flows

> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-08
> **Purpose**: Define user journeys and navigation patterns
> **Design Principle**: Minimize clicks, maximize context

---

## Navigation Philosophy

### Core Principles
1. **Context preservation**: User never loses their place in the 12-year timeline
2. **Progressive disclosure**: Details on demand, overview first
3. **Gestalt proximity**: Related actions grouped together
4. **Minimal navigation depth**: Maximum 2 clicks to any feature

### Navigation Types
- **Spatial navigation**: Pan/zoom in canvas (primary)
- **Sidebar navigation**: Fleet hierarchy (secondary)
- **Action navigation**: Task interactions (contextual)
- **Global navigation**: Masthead links (persistent)

---

## User Journeys

### 1. Daily Planning Check

**User**: Maintenance Planner
**Goal**: Check today's scheduled work and identify issues

```
Journey Flow:
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 1. OPEN APP                                                          │
│    └── Dashboard loads at today's date, MESO zoom level             │
│                                                                      │
│ 2. REVIEW KPIs                                                       │
│    ├── Check Conflicts card (red if > 0)                            │
│    ├── Check Fit % (should be > 85%)                                │
│    └── Check Mileage variance                                       │
│                                                                      │
│ 3. IF CONFLICTS > 0:                                                 │
│    ├── Click Conflicts KPI card                                     │
│    ├── View conflict list (sorted by severity)                      │
│    ├── Click first conflict                                         │
│    ├── Canvas pans to conflicting tasks                             │
│    └── Task Detail Panel shows conflict info                        │
│                                                                      │
│ 4. RESOLVE CONFLICT:                                                 │
│    ├── Enter Sandbox mode (click toggle)                            │
│    ├── Drag task to new date                                        │
│    ├── Verify conflict resolved (count decreases)                   │
│    ├── Toggle back to Baseline (changes preserved in sandbox)       │
│    └── Export scenario for approval                                 │
│                                                                      │
│ 5. EXIT                                                              │
│    └── Changes auto-saved in sandbox                                │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Time: < 5 minutes
Clicks: 6-8
```

### 2. Train Investigation

**User**: Fleet Manager
**Goal**: Investigate why a specific train is underperforming

```
Journey Flow:
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 1. SEARCH TRAIN                                                      │
│    ├── Click search box in sidebar                                  │
│    ├── Type train ID (e.g., "5003")                                 │
│    └── Press Enter or click result                                  │
│                                                                      │
│ 2. CANVAS PANS                                                       │
│    ├── Canvas smoothly pans to train row                            │
│    ├── Train row highlighted                                        │
│    └── Current zoom preserved                                       │
│                                                                      │
│ 3. VIEW TRAIN HISTORY                                                │
│    ├── Zoom out to see more context (scroll wheel)                  │
│    ├── See maintenance pattern over time                            │
│    └── Identify gaps or clusters                                    │
│                                                                      │
│ 4. CHECK MILEAGE                                                     │
│    ├── Click train row header (or sidebar item)                     │
│    ├── Train Detail Panel opens                                     │
│    ├── See mileage: actual vs predicted                             │
│    ├── See variance trend (sparkline)                               │
│    └── See last/next maintenance dates                              │
│                                                                      │
│ 5. DRILL DOWN                                                        │
│    ├── Click "View Full History" in panel                           │
│    ├── Opens Train Detail View                                      │
│    ├── Full mileage chart                                           │
│    └── Complete maintenance history                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Time: < 3 minutes
Clicks: 4-5
```

### 3. What-If Scenario Planning

**User**: Operations Manager
**Goal**: Test impact of rescheduling major overhaul

```
Journey Flow:
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 1. ENTER SANDBOX MODE                                                │
│    ├── Click mode toggle (Baseline → Sandbox)                       │
│    ├── UI shows violet accents                                      │
│    ├── "Sandbox Mode" indicator visible                             │
│    └── All changes tracked in modification log                      │
│                                                                      │
│ 2. NAVIGATE TO OVERHAUL                                              │
│    ├── Use sidebar filter: Type = Overhaul                          │
│    ├── Or search by task ID                                         │
│    ├── Click task in list or canvas                                 │
│    └── Canvas centers on task                                       │
│                                                                      │
│ 3. RESCHEDULE TASK                                                   │
│    ├── Task Detail Panel opens                                      │
│    ├── Click "Reschedule" button                                    │
│    ├── Date picker modal appears                                    │
│    ├── Select new start date                                        │
│    ├── System shows impact preview:                                 │
│    │   - New conflicts introduced?                                  │
│    │   - Resource availability?                                     │
│    │   - Mileage impact?                                            │
│    └── Click "Apply" to confirm                                     │
│                                                                      │
│ 4. REVIEW IMPACT                                                     │
│    ├── Canvas updates with new position                             │
│    ├── KPIs recalculate in real-time                                │
│    ├── Modification count shows "1 change"                          │
│    └── Can continue making more changes                             │
│                                                                      │
│ 5. COMPARE OR REVERT                                                 │
│    ├── Option A: Toggle to Baseline to compare                      │

# MMS Chart Types Specification > **Last Updated**: 2026-01-08 > **Purpose**: Define all visualization types and their implementation > **Key Principle**: Right chart for right insight --- ## Chart Type Summary | Chart | Purpose | Location | LOD | |-------|---------|----------|-----| | Calendar Heatmap | Task density overview | MACRO layer | < 0.5x | | Task Blocks | Individual task visualization | MESO layer | 0.5x - 2.0x | | Task Cards | Detailed task info | MICRO layer | > 2.0x | | Marey Diagram | Train movement over time | Optional view | Any | | Resource Histogram | Man-hours/bay utilization | Resource panel | N/A | | Variance Line Chart | Mileage actual vs predicted | Mileage dashboard | N/A | | KPI Cards | Summary metrics | KPI bar | N/A | | Sparklines | Inline trends | Panels, tables | N/A | | Utilization Gauge | Resource usage % | Resource panel | N/A | --- ## 1. Calendar Heatmap (MACRO Layer) ### Purpose Show 12-year × 128-train overview as color-coded density map. ### Visual Design ``` Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep Oct Nov Dec 2024 ░░░ ▒▒▒ ▓▓▓ ██ ▒▒▒ ░░░ ░░░ ░░░ ▒▒▒ ▓▓▓ ██ ▓▓▓ 2025 ░░░ ▒▒▒ ▒▒▒ ▓▓▓ ██ ▓▓▓ ▒▒▒ ░░░ ░░░ ▒▒▒ ▓▓▓ ██ 2026 ▒▒▒ ░░░ ░░░ ▒▒▒ ▓▓▓ ██ ▓▓▓ ▒▒▒ ░░░ ░░░ ▒▒▒ ▓▓▓ ... 2035 ░░░ ▒▒▒ ▓▓▓ ▓▓▓ ▒▒▒ ░░░ ░░░ ▒▒▒ ▓▓▓ ██ ▓▓▓ ▒▒▒ Legend: ░ Low (0-5 tasks/cell) → ██ High (20+ tasks/cell) ``` ### Color Scale (Viridis) ```typescript const viridis = [ [253, 231, 37], // Yellow (0 tasks) [122, 209, 81], // Light green [34, 168, 132], // Teal [42, 120, 142], // Blue-teal [65, 68, 135], // Purple-blue [68, 1, 84], // Dark purple (max tasks) ]; ``` ### Implementation ```typescript // Cell dimensions at MACRO zoom const CELL_WIDTH = 30; // days in month const CELL_HEIGHT = 1; // one train per row // Density calculation function calculateCellDensity(tasks: Task[], cellBounds: Bounds): number { return tasks.filter(t => isInBounds(t, cellBounds)).length; } // Color mapping function densityToColor(density: number, maxDensity: number): RGB { const normalized = Math.min(density / maxDensity, 1); const index = Math.floor(normalized * (viridis.length - 1)); return viridis[index]; } ``` ### Interactions - **Hover**: Show tooltip with month, task count, man-hours - **Click**: Zoom to MESO level for that month/train range - **Double-click**: Zoom to week level ### Gestalt Application - **Similarity**: Same colors = same density - **Continuity**: Left-to-right = time progression - **Closure**: Grid cells clearly defined --- ## 2. Task Blocks (MESO Layer) ### Purpose Show individual tasks as colored blocks on timeline. ### Visual Design ``` Train 5001: ▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░▓▓▓░░░░░▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░ Train 5002: ░░░░▓▓▓▓░░░░░░░░▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░░░▓▓▓ Train 5003: ▓▓▓░░░░░░▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░░░▓▓▓▓▓░░░░ Jan─────────────Feb─────────────Mar Legend: Different colors = different task types ``` ### Color Palette (PatternFly accessible) ```typescript const TASK_COLORS = { preventive: [0.20, 0.60, 0.86], // #3498DB Blue corrective: [0.90, 0.50, 0.13], // #E67E22 Orange inspection: [0.16, 0.50, 0.73], // #2980B9 Dark blue overhaul: [0.60, 0.35, 0.71], // #9B59B6 Purple conflict: [0.90, 0.30, 0.30], // #E74C3C Red selected: [0.02, 0.71, 0.83], // #06B6D4 Cyan }; ``` ### Implementation ```typescript // Using React Three Fiber InstancedMesh {/* Each instance = one task block */} // Position calculation function taskToPosition(task: Task): Vector3 { return new Vector3( task.startDay * DAY_WIDTH, task.trainId * TRAIN_HEIGHT, task.status === 'conflict' ? 1 : 0 // Z-order for conflicts ); } // Size calculation function taskToScale(task: Task): Vector3 { return new Vector3( task.duration * DAY_WIDTH, TASK_HEIGHT, 1 ); } ``` ### Visual States | State | Visual Treatment | |-------|-----------------| | Normal | Task type color | | Hovered | 20% brightness increase | | Selected | Cyan border (#06B6D4) | | Conflict | Red color + z=1 (front) | | Modified (sandbox) | Dashed border | ### Interactions - **Hover**: Highlight block, show tooltip - **Click**: Select task, open detail panel - **Shift+Click**: Add to multi-selection - **Drag** (sandbox): Move task to new date --- ## 3. Task Cards (MICRO Layer) ### Purpose Show detailed task information with labels and actions. ### Visual Design ``` ┌────────────────────────────────┐ │ 🔧 PM-5001-0042 │ ├────────────────────────────────┤ │ Preventive Maintenance │ │ Train: 5001 (Kinki Sharyo) │ │ Mar 15-18, 2024 (3 days) │ │ ───────────────────────────── │ │ Man-hours: 48h │ │ [Reschedule] [Details] │ └────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Implementation ```tsx // HTML overlay approach for MICRO level function TaskCard({ task, position, scale }) { return (
{task.name}
Train: {task.trainId}
{formatDateRange(task.start, task.end)}
); } ``` ### Performance Limit - Render max 50 cards (visible viewport only) - Beyond viewport: fall back to MESO blocks --- ## 4. Marey Diagram (Alternative View) ### Purpose Visualize train movements and identify delays/conflicts. ### Visual Design ``` Station │ ────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────── Terminal A │ ╲ ╱╲ ╱╲ ╱╲ │ ╲ ╱ ╲ ╱ ╲ ╱ ╲ Station B │ ╲ ╱ ╲ ╱ ╲ ╱ ╲ │ ╲ ╱ ╲╱ ╲ ╱ ╲ Station C │ ╲╱ ╲ ╲╱ ╲ │ ╱╲ ╲ ╱╲ ╲ Station D │ ╱ ╲ ╲ ╱ ╲ ╲ │ ╱ ╲ ╲ ╱ ╲ ╲ Terminal B │ ╱ ╲ ╲╱ ╲ ╲ ────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────── │ 6:00 8:00 10:00 12:00 14:00 ↘ = Train going A→B (slope = speed) ↗ = Train going B→A X = Trains crossing (same point = same station at same time) Horizontal = Train stopped at station ``` ### Key Insights from Marey - **Slope = Speed**: Steeper line = faster train - **Crossing lines**: Trains pass each other - **Horizontal section**: Stop duration at station - **Gaps**: Service gaps or delays ### When to Use - Operations planning view - Delay propagation analysis - Service pattern visualization - NOT for maintenance scheduling (use Gantt) ### Implementation Reference - [D3.js Marey's Trains](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains) - [JointJS Marey Chart](https://www.jointjs.com/demos/marey-chart) --- ## 5. Resource Histogram ### Purpose Show man-hours/bay utilization vs capacity over time. ### Visual Design ``` 100% ┬────────────────────────────── overload zone │ ▓▓▓▓ ▓▓▓▓ 90% ┼────▓▓▓▓──────────▓▓▓▓──── target max │ ▓▓▓▓▓▓ ▓▓▓▓▓▓ 80% ┼──▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓──────▓▓▓▓▓▓──── target min │▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ 70% ┼▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ │▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ 60% ┼▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ └──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴── W1 W2 W3 W4 W5 W6 W7 W8 W9 Legend: ───── Target band (80-90%) ▓▓▓▓▓ Scheduled utilization ░░░░░ Available capacity ``` ### Color Coding - **Green** (< 80%): Under-utilized - **Blue** (80-90%): Optimal zone - **Yellow** (90-100%): Near capacity - **Red** (> 100%): Overloaded ### Implementation ```typescript function ResourceHistogram({ data, targetMin, targetMax }) { return ( ); } ``` --- ## 6. Variance Line Chart ### Purpose Track actual vs predicted mileage over time. ### Visual Design ``` Variance % +15% ┬ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ critical threshold │ ╱╲ +10% ┼ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─╱──╲─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ warning threshold │ ╱ ╲ ╱╲ +5% ┼ ─ ─ ─ ╱ ─ ─ ─╲─ ─╱─ ╲ ─ ─ normal threshold │ ╱ ╲╱ ╲ 0% ┼═════╱══════════════════╲════ baseline │ ╱ ╲ -5% ┼ ─ ╱ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ╲ ─ normal threshold │ ╱ ╲ -10% ┼ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ╲ warning threshold └──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴── 1 5 10 15 20 25 30 (days) Legend: ════ Actual variance (thick line) ─ ─ Threshold lines (dashed) ░░░ Warning zone (shaded) ``` ### Threshold Coloring - **Normal zone** (±5%): No shading - **Warning zone** (5-10%): Yellow shading - **Critical zone** (>10%): Red shading --- ## 7. KPI Cards ### Purpose At-a-glance summary metrics. ### Visual Design ``` ┌───────────────────┐ │ 📊 METRIC LABEL │ │ │ │ 5,348 │ ← Large number │ │ │ ▲ +15 vs yesterday│ ← Delta with trend arrow │ ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░ 85% │ ← Optional progress bar └───────────────────┘ ``` ### Card Types | Card | Metric | Delta | Progress | Color Logic | |------|--------|-------|----------|-------------| | Tasks | Total count | vs yesterday | N/A | Neutral | | Conflicts | Conflict count | vs yesterday | N/A | Red if > 0 | | Fit % | Fit trains % | vs yesterday | Yes | Green > 85% | | Mileage | Fleet variance | vs predicted | N/A | Green ±5% | ### Implementation ```tsx function KPICard({ label, value, delta, progress, status }) { const statusColor = { success: 'var(--pf-global--success-color--100)', warning: 'var(--pf-global--warning-color--100)', danger: 'var(--pf-global--danger-color--100)', }; return (
{formatNumber(value)}
{delta && (
0 ? 'green' : 'red' }}> {delta > 0 ? '▲' : '▼'} {Math.abs(delta)}
)} {progress && ( )} ); } ``` --- ## 8. Sparklines ### Purpose Inline mini-charts for trend visualization in tables/panels. ### Visual Design ``` Train 5001 ▂▃▅▆█▆▅▃▂▃▄▅▆▇█▇▆ +2.3% Train 5002 ▇▆▅▄▃▂▁▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█▇▆ -1.8% Train 5003 ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█▇▆▅▄▃▂▁▂▃ +0.5% ``` ### Implementation ```tsx function Sparkline({ data, width = 100, height = 20 }) { const normalized = normalizeData(data, 0, height); const points = normalized.map((y, i) => `${i * (width / data.length)},${height - y}`).join(' '); return ( ); } ``` --- ## 9. Utilization Gauge ### Purpose Show current resource utilization as percentage. ### Visual Design ``` ┌─────────────────────────┐ │ 87% │ │ ████████████░░░░░░░░░ │ │ ▲ │ │ Target: 80-90% │ └─────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Color Logic - **< 70%**: Yellow (under-utilized) - **70-90%**: Green (optimal) - **90-100%**: Orange (near capacity) - **> 100%**: Red (overloaded) --- ## LOD Transition Formulas ### Zoom to LOD Mapping ```typescript function zoomToLOD(zoom: number): 'MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO' { if (zoom < 0.5) return 'MACRO'; if (zoom > 2.0) return 'MICRO'; return 'MESO'; } ``` ### Crossfade Opacity (Hermite Smoothstep) ```typescript function smoothstep(edge0: number, edge1: number, x: number): number { const t = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (x - edge0) / (edge1 - edge0))); return t * t * (3 - 2 * t); } function getLODOpacities(zoom: number): { macro: number; meso: number; micro: number } { const MACRO_END = 0.5; const MESO_START = 0.4; const MESO_END = 2.2; const MICRO_START = 1.8; return { macro: 1 - smoothstep(MESO_START, MACRO_END, zoom), meso: Math.min( smoothstep(MESO_START, MACRO_END, zoom), 1 - smoothstep(MICRO_START, MESO_END, zoom) ), micro: smoothstep(MICRO_START, MESO_END, zoom), }; } ``` ### Visual Transition ``` Zoom: 0.1x ────────── 0.5x ────────── 2.0x ────────── 4.0x MACRO: ████████████████████▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ MESO: ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░▓▓▓▓████████████████████▓▓▓▓░░░░░░ MICRO: ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░▓▓▓▓▓▓████████████ ████ = Full opacity (1.0) ▓▓▓▓ = Transition (0.5) ░░░░ = Hidden (0.0) ``` --- ## Accessibility Considerations ### Color Blindness - Use Viridis colormap (designed for accessibility) - Add patterns/textures in addition to color - Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio minimum ### Screen Readers - All charts have aria-labels - Data tables as alternative to visual charts - Keyboard navigation for chart elements ### Motion Sensitivity - Reduce motion option disables transitions - No auto-playing animations - User controls animation speed

# Architecture Decision Records - MMS (Metro Maintenance Scheduler)

> **Document Purpose**: Consolidated architectural decisions from 6+ development attempts.
> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-07
> **Status**: Reference document for implementation

---

## ADR-001: Rendering Engine - WebGL via PixiJS v8

**Decision:** Use PixiJS v8 for WebGL rendering instead of DOM/SVG

**Alternatives Rejected:**
- DOM rendering (HTML elements)
- SVG
- Canvas 2D
- React-only rendering

**Reasoning:**
Due to the density of information (500,000+ data points for a 12-year period), traditional DOM (HTML/SVG) is unfeasible. PixiJS is the most performant 2D engine in the market for batch rendering of geometric primitives, essential for the 12-year Heatmap.

User explicitly stated: *"o projeto deve ser impressionante, como o google maps"* (the project must be impressive, like Google Maps).

**Tradeoffs:**
| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| 60fps for 50k+ items | Requires specialized WebGL knowledge |
| GPU-accelerated batch rendering | Requires hybrid architecture for text |
| Native support for zoom/pan | Steeper learning curve |

**Who Decided:** User
**Confidence:** High

---

## ADR-002: Navigation Physics - pixi-viewport

**Decision:** Use pixi-viewport for Google Maps-style navigation physics

**Alternatives Rejected:**
- Custom pan/zoom implementation
- DOM scrolling
- Other viewport libraries

**Reasoning:**
Implements natively the navigation physics required (Pan, Zoom, Inertia, Pinch-to-zoom) matching the Google Maps style that user explicitly requested. Battle-tested physics implementation saves months of development.

**Tradeoffs:**
| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Ready-made smooth physics | Additional dependency |
| Handles edge cases | Less customization flexibility |
| Touch/gesture support | |

**Who Decided:** User + Assistant
**Confidence:** High

---

## ADR-003: Virtual Scrolling Approach - Math/Physics Based

**Decision:** Virtual Scroller based on math/physics instead of DOM scrolling

**Alternatives Rejected:**
- Ghost Div Pattern (native DOM scroll)
- react-window
- react-virtualized

**Reasoning:**
DOM scrolling has limitations for fractal zoom. Math-based approach allows seamless integration with WebGL camera and 8 semantic zoom levels.

**Critical Failure Reference:** Previous attempt used Ghost Div and failed. User explicitly stated: *"DOM Scrolling -> Virtual Scroller (baseado em matematica/fisica)"*

**Tradeoffs:**
| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Infinite scrolling capability | More complex implementation |
| WebGL camera integration | No native scrollbar |
| Seamless fractal zoom | Custom physics required |

**Who Decided:** User
**Confidence:** High

---

## ADR-004: Data Structures - Structure of Arrays (SoA)

**Decision:** Structure of Arrays (SoA) with TypedArrays instead of Array of Objects (AoS)

**Alternatives Rejected:**
